about.metaDescription: Frontend engineer Younghoon Kim, working on repeatable operations tooling, verification flows, and state reproduction with React and TypeScript.
about.intro: I build internal web tools with React and TypeScript so operators, QA, and support teams can repeat work with stable state, clear input contracts, and visible verification steps. Recent work includes FC Online account lookup and edit flows, server-state refresh, gRPC test UI, Content Manager validation, and build cost reduction.

items.developerExperience: Flows that let operators and QA reopen the same state
items.reliableInterfaces: Input contracts shared by forms, URLs, and test tools
items.runtimeBehavior: Regressions caused by browser and runtime differences
items.openSource: Open-source PRs that landed in official releases

ea.highlight1: Aligned account lookup and edit screens around URL state and React Query cache so QA can reopen the same screen state.
ea.highlight2: Kept forms, URL params, and internal validation tools on the same TypeScript and Zod input contract; refreshed nine stale-after-mutation flows.
ea.highlight3: Grouped Content Manager validation and a gRPC test UI inside the operations tool; reduced entry JS gzip from 195.9KB to 111.1KB through Vite 8, chunk, and preload changes.

nhn.highlight1: Preserved filters and scroll context across list, detail, and reservation screens with React Query cache.
nhn.highlight2: Reduced loop-swipe rendering and painting events from 156 per second to 32 per second.
nhn.highlight3: Mitigated long-list crashes on older iPhone Safari and raised sub-20fps sections to an average above 50fps.

pandora.highlight1: Refetched payment and membership state after app payment-completion notifications to prevent duplicate requests and stale status.
pandora.highlight2: Designed a draft autosave flow with a backend developer so long posts survive app exits and back navigation.
pandora.highlight3: Separated public search-entry pages behind a Next.js SSR boundary and migrated 100+ files from JS to TS and React 16 to 18 by feature.

openSource.heading: Merged open-source work
openSource.lead: PRs that landed in official releases across performance, SSR regression tests, runtime compatibility, type safety, and CI stability.

query.pr1Title: Optimized useQueries from O(N²) to O(N)
query.pr1Desc: Reduced function calls in the 500-query benchmark from 4,500 to 2,000 (about -55%).
query.pr2Desc: Resolved stale UI after page refresh.
query.pr3Desc: Stabilized a mismatch in the Nx Cloud distributed execution config.

router.pr2Desc: Added safe handling for session save failures in private mode.
router.pr3Desc: Added regression tests for SSR environments.
router.pr5Desc: Mitigated CSS preload failures and cache conflicts in CDN CORS environments.

rhf.pr1Desc: Resolved the never-lock issue and added type tests.

tsr.pr1Desc: Changed 500 to 404 behavior and added tests.

stack.heading: What I reach for
stack.line: React, TypeScript, and Next.js as a base. TanStack Query, React Router, and Zustand for state and routing. Vite, Vitest, Playwright, and ESLint to keep build and tests honest. Node, Fastify, Prisma, and tRPC when I need a backend.

connect.heading: Let's connect
connect.description: Available for concrete conversations about operations tooling, React/TypeScript, and open-source PRs.
